entries.text: Tyrannosaurus rex, or T. rex, was a big meat-eating dinosaur. It lived about 68 to 66 million years ago. It had huge teeth and strong legs, and it walked on two legs.
question_hints: What did T rex look like

entries.text: T. rex was one of the biggest meat-eating dinosaurs. It could be about 12 metres long and 4 metres tall. Its head was very big and its tail helped it balance.
T. rex had about 60 teeth. Each tooth could be as long as your hand. The teeth were sharp and curved, good for eating other dinosaurs. New teeth grew when old ones broke.
question_hints: Did T rex have sharp teeth
How many teeth did T rex have

entries.text: T. rex had very short arms with two fingers on each. Scientists are not sure what they were for. They might have helped when getting up from the ground or holding food.
question_hints: Why did T rex have small arms
How many fingers did T rex have

entries.text: Dinosaurs lived a very long time ago. They were on Earth for more than 150 million years. The last dinosaurs died out about 66 million years ago. People did not live at the same time as dinosaurs.
Many dinosaurs ate plants. They are called plant-eaters or herbivores. Some had long necks to reach tall trees. Others had thick bodies and armour or spikes to stay safe.
Stegosaurus was a plant-eating dinosaur with big flat plates on its back and spikes on its tail. It lived in North America. The plates might have helped it stay warm or look big to other dinosaurs.
Triceratops had three horns on its face and a big bony frill around its neck. It ate plants and could use its horns to defend itself. It lived at the same time as T. rex.
We learn about dinosaurs from fossils. Fossils are the hardened remains or prints of animals and plants that lived long ago. Bones, teeth, and footprints can become fossils in rocks.
question_hints: How do we know about dinosaurs
